name: Toledo Metro
description_short: Metro Credit Union is a Massachusetts-based credit union offering checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services across 18 branches in the Boston metro area.
description_long:
Metro Credit Union is a federally chartered credit union headquartered in Chelsea, Massachusetts, serving members throughout the greater Boston region. The organization operates as a member-owned financial cooperative with routing number 211381990 and maintains a primary mailing address at PO Box 9100, Chelsea, MA 02150-9100. The credit union has expanded its physical footprint to include 18 branch locations across Massachusetts communities including Boston, Dorchester, Burlington, Framingham, Lawrence, Lynn, Medford, Melrose, Newton, Peabody, Reading, Salem, and Tewksbury.

Metro Credit Union offers comprehensive banking services including membership accounts, checking and savings products, loan origination and servicing, and digital banking through iBanking and mobile app platforms. Members can access online account management, loan payment processing, and ATM services. The credit union provides virtual assistant support through "Chelsea," an automated system capable of handling routine banking transactions and member inquiries, supplemented by human Member Service Agents available during extended hours including Saturday service.

The credit union distinguishes itself through extended customer service hours (Monday-Friday 8 AM to 6 PM, Saturday 8 AM to 1 PM) and 24/7 digital banking support. Their branch network is concentrated in the Boston metropolitan area, providing physical locations for members who prefer in-person banking. The organization emphasizes accessibility through multiple contact channels including phone (877.MY.METRO / 877.696.3876), email, and a digital branch option for remote consultations.

As a credit union, Metro operates on a not-for-profit, member-owned basis rather than generating shareholder returns. However, the available website content does not disclose membership requirements, fee structures, interest rates, loan terms, or competitive positioning relative to other financial institutions. No information is provided regarding asset size, member count, regulatory examination history, or specific product offerings beyond the mention of loans and basic banking services. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal and consumer loan origination and servicing
iBanking online platform with account management
pros: 18 physical branch locations across greater Boston metro area with extended hours including Saturday banking
Extended customer service hours: Monday-Friday until 6 PM, Saturday until 1 PM, plus 24/7 iBanking support
Virtual assistant "Chelsea" available after-hours for routine transactions to reduce wait times
Nationwide ATM access and digital banking through mobile app and online iBanking platform
Member-owned credit union structure operates on not-for-profit basis
Digital branch option allowing remote consultations with agents
Transparent routing number and mailing address readily available
cons: Website provides no information on membership eligibility requirements or application process
Interest rates, fee schedules, and loan terms not disclosed on available web pages
Limited to Massachusetts member base with no indication of service expansion or remote membership options
No details provided on specific loan products, credit card offerings, or savings account types
Lack of publicly available competitive comparison or regulatory examination summaries
best_for: Boston metro area residents seeking local credit union banking with branch access
Members who value extended business hours including Saturday availability
Customers preferring member-owned cooperative banking structure over for-profit banks
People seeking 24/7 digital banking options through mobile app and online platforms
diagnosis: Metro Credit Union is best suited for greater Boston area residents seeking accessible, member-owned banking with physical branch locations and extended service hours. The primary caveat is that critical product details—including membership requirements, fees, interest rates, and specific loan terms—are not disclosed on their public-facing website, requiring prospective members to contact the credit union directly for detailed information.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
